comments.text:
👇 Everything we curated for this Yangtze episode. Books, cookbooks, history, and travel resources.

*📚 BROWSE THE YANGTZE COLLECTION*
  ◦ Every book, cookbook, and cultural item we curated for this episode → https://amzn.to/3QfJNdb

*🛍 SEE OUR FULL W.O.E. STOREFRONT*
  ◦ Every destination Collection in one place → https://www.amazon.com/shop/whereonearth

*🎟 TOURS AND EXPERIENCES ALONG THE YANGTZE*
  ◦ Yangtze River cruises and Three Gorges experiences → https://shorturl.at/dBk2g
  ◦ Browse all China experiences → https://shorturl.at/bZkIn

*📖 PLAN YOUR YANGTZE TRIP*
  ◦ The River at the Center of the World by Simon Winchester → https://amzn.to/4dDqdAC
  ◦ Lonely Planet Mandarin Phrasebook and Dictionary → https://amzn.to/4fhXwdO

*📚 GO DEEPER INTO CHINA*
  ◦ The Search for Modern China by Jonathan Spence → https://amzn.to/49vJ6D6
  ◦ River Town: Two Years on the Yangtze by Peter Hessler → https://amzn.to/4adsEHJ

*🍲 BRING THE YANGTZE HOME*
  ◦ The Food of Sichuan by Fuchsia Dunlop → https://amzn.to/4dGSeao
  ◦ Handmade Yixing purple clay teapot → https://amzn.to/4nVwACC

comments.author: @RealLifeGlobalDocumentary
comments.text:
The Yangtze is the longest river in Asia 🌊
Stretching over 6,300 km (3,900+ miles), it flows entirely within China and has influenced the lives of hundreds of millions of people for thousands of years.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: The scale of it is almost hard to process — a river that long, flowing through so much history. Thank you for watching. 🙏

comments.author: @miabhlub63
comments.text: Spectacular views. Beautiful scenery

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Thank you for watching. 🌿

comments.author: @bonanap7183
comments.text: world over population

comments.author: @ZbyszekBECKER
comments.text: The concluding remarks are pure propaganda with no bearing in facts! The very story of recurring huge floods interspersed with prolonged droughts - the film admits that - prove that climate was always changing and fluctuating, nothing new under the sun. Some of the footage is breathtaking, some quite disappointing, repeating or nonexisting (e.g. 3 Gorges - not a single shot). In a way rather so so...

comments.author: @tonics7121
comments.text: This is a VIDEO.  To repeatedly show city when talking about great beauty of river is just plain disrespectful. I'm outta here.

comments.author: @FionaStewart-kt8vz
comments.text: I feel very sad watching this. Our ability to learn from our mistakes.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: The Yangtze carries centuries of exactly that — the grandeur and the cost arriving in the same frame. Thank you for watching. 🌿

comments.author: @LongHồng-h9k
comments.text: What a stunning journey from the glaciers of Tibet to the skyscrapers of Shanghai! This documentary perfectly captures both the life-giving beauty and the destructive power of Asia’s longest river.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: The glacier-to-skyline progression is one of the most dramatic geographic journeys on earth — and the river carries all of it. What part of the film stayed with you most?

would you have most wanted to see explored?

comments.author: @LiliWang-l1m
comments.text: After a few years of forbidding of fishing and other efforts of protection for the Yangze river, lots species of water creatures are back including the baiji dolphin luckily.

replies.author: @Earlybird-g4e
replies.text: But western media would not mentioned that

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: The recovery of the river's ecosystem is one of the most hopeful chapters in its story — what the fishing ban has quietly started is remarkable. A long journey still ahead. 🌿

comments.author: @WJKPhD
comments.text:
Lots of very interesting, well researched history. Very nice photography. Thank you, Where on Earth! 
However, this is a pro-environment, anti-industrial civilization narration. It begrudgingly admits The Three Gorges Dam works, but implies it may fail as conditions change. Not a lot of pictures of the dam. Guess they couldn’t get permission. Officials must have read the script. Nice voice.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: The Three Gorges coverage is a fair note — a structure of that scale deserved more time in the frame. Something to build on in the next pass. Thank you for the thoughtful read.
